description: raws includes null-valued fields in JSON output; AWS CLI omits them. Affects s3api, dynamodb, lambda, and likely all services. Example: get-bucket-versioning returns {"Status": null, "MFADelete": null} instead of {}

verify.expect: JSON output should omit null fields, matching AWS CLI behavior

notes: Found by validation agent. Systemic issue across all protocols. AWS CLI strips null values from JSON responses.

description: raws error messages are missing the 'aws: [ERROR]: ' prefix that the AWS CLI prepends to all error output. Example: raws outputs 'An error occurred (X)...' but AWS CLI outputs 'aws: [ERROR]: An error occurred (X)...'

verify.expect: Error should be prefixed with 'raws: [ERROR]: ' (or equivalent branding)

notes: Found by validation agent. Affects all error output across all services.

description: raws lacks client-side parameter validation. When required params are missing, raws sends the request to the server and gets a server-side error (exit 254/255), while AWS CLI validates client-side with 'the following arguments are required: --param-name' (exit 252). Affects all commands with required parameters.

verify.expect: Should show 'the following arguments are required: --role-arn, --role-session-name' with exit code 252

notes: Found by validation agent. This is the single largest source of test failures across all services. AWS CLI validates required params before making API calls.

description: raws uses exit code 255 for most errors, but AWS CLI uses specific exit codes: 252 for parameter validation errors, 253 for no-credentials errors, 254 for service errors. raws should match these exit code conventions.

verify.expect: Exit code 252 for missing params

verify.run: env -u AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID -u AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY -u AWS_SESSION_TOKEN raws sts get-caller-identity --region us-east-1 2>&1; echo $?
verify.expect: Exit code 253 for no credentials

notes: Found by validation agent. Exit codes differ for parameter validation (raws: 255, aws: 252) and no-credentials (raws: 255, aws: 253).

description: raws table output renders nested objects and arrays as inline JSON strings in a flat table row. AWS CLI renders them as properly formatted nested sub-tables with double-pipe borders. Also, raws omits the top-level operation name header row.

verify.expect: Nested objects like CidrBlockAssociationSet should render as sub-tables, not inline JSON

notes: Found by validation agent. Affects ec2 describe-vpcs, iam list-users, kms list-keys, and any response with nested structures.

description: raws produces no output for empty table results. AWS CLI shows a minimal table header even when results are empty.

verify.expect: Should show table header even if no instances exist

notes: Found by validation agent. Affects dynamodb list-tables, ec2 describe-instances, lambda list-functions when results are empty.

description: raws does not support the --version flag. Running 'raws --version' produces a clap argument parsing error instead of a version string.

verify.expect: Should output a version string like 'raws/0.1.0 ...'

description: EC2 describe-images --owners self and describe-snapshots --owner-ids self ignore the filter parameter entirely, returning ALL public images/snapshots (79K+ items) instead of only self-owned ones. The EC2 protocol list parameter serialization (Owner.N) is not working.

verify.run: raws ec2 describe-images --owners self --region us-east-1 --output json 2>&1 | head -5
verify.expect: Should return only self-owned images (likely empty list), not all public images

verify.run: raws ec2 describe-snapshots --owner-ids self --region us-east-1 --output json 2>&1 | head -5
verify.expect: Should return only self-owned snapshots, not all public snapshots

notes: Found by validation agent. Critical performance and correctness bug. AWS CLI correctly filters; raws returns unfiltered results.
Fixed: Added serialize_ec2_request() with EC2-specific list serialization (no .member. wrapper) and locationName capitalization. Also made parse_operation_args() model-aware for list/map type params.

description: IAM list-roles and get-account-authorization-details return AssumeRolePolicyDocument and PolicyDocument as raw URL-encoded strings (e.g., %7B%22Version%22...) instead of decoded JSON objects. AWS CLI properly decodes them.

verify.run: raws iam list-roles --region us-east-1 --output json 2>&1 | head -30
verify.expect: AssumeRolePolicyDocument should be a JSON object, not a URL-encoded string

notes: Found by validation agent. IAM returns URL-encoded policy documents in its responses; AWS CLI decodes them before displaying.

description: IAM list-policies with any optional parameter (--scope Local, --max-items, --path-prefix, --only-attached) causes a SignatureDoesNotMatch error: 'Credential should be scoped to a valid region'. Works fine without optional params. Likely a query protocol signing bug when serializing optional parameters on the IAM global endpoint.

verify.expect: Should return policies scoped to Local, not a SignatureDoesNotMatch error

notes: Found by validation agent. This breaks many IAM commands that use optional filters.

description: S3 operations on buckets in different regions return PermanentRedirect errors instead of transparently following the redirect. AWS CLI handles S3 redirects automatically. Affects s3api get-bucket-versioning, list-objects-v2, get-bucket-acl and s3 ls s3://<bucket>/ on cross-region buckets.

verify.expect: Should list objects, not return HTTP 301 redirect error

notes: Found by validation agent. S3 requires following 301 redirects to the correct regional endpoint. raws may be using path-style URLs instead of virtual-hosted-style.

description: s3api head-bucket fails on buckets in us-east-1 with 'An error occurred (Unknown)' while AWS CLI succeeds. May be related to the S3 global endpoint vs regional endpoint handling for us-east-1.

verify.expect: Should succeed (no output) like AWS CLI

description: s3api list-directory-buckets returns all buckets (same as list-buckets) instead of only S3 Express directory buckets. AWS CLI correctly returns an empty list when no directory buckets exist.

verify.expect: Should return empty Buckets list (no directory buckets), not all regular buckets

notes: Found by validation agent. raws appears to route this to the same endpoint as list-buckets.

description: s3api get-bucket-acl response is missing the 'Type' field in the Grantee object. AWS CLI returns {"Type": "CanonicalUser", "DisplayName": ..., "ID": ...} but raws omits the Type field.

verify.expect: Grantee objects should include 'Type': 'CanonicalUser'

notes: Found by validation agent. The Type field may be an XML attribute that raws is not parsing.

description: When AWS returns errors with extra fields (e.g., Type: Sender, BucketName), AWS CLI appends 'Additional error details:\nType: Sender' to the error message. raws omits this extra detail.

verify.expect: Error message should include 'Additional error details' section

notes: Found by validation agent. Affects STS and S3 error responses that include extra context fields.

description: When no credentials are available, raws error message format differs from AWS CLI. raws: 'Failed to resolve AWS credentials: No credentials found...' (exit 255). AWS: 'aws: [ERROR]: Unable to locate credentials' (exit 253).

verify.run: env -u AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID -u AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY -u AWS_SESSION_TOKEN raws sts get-caller-identity --region us-east-1 2>&1; echo $?
verify.expect: Should say 'Unable to locate credentials' with exit code 253

description: When using a nonexistent profile, raws outputs 'The config profile (X) could not be found' but AWS CLI prefixes it with 'aws: [ERROR]: '. The core message matches but the prefix is missing.

verify.expect: Error should be prefixed with 'raws: [ERROR]: ' or similar

notes: Found by validation agent. Related to the systemic error prefix issue.

description: The 's3 website' subcommand is not implemented in raws. Running it returns 'Unknown s3 subcommand'.

verify.expect: Should show usage/help for s3 website command
